metadata.title: About · Wooden Ren Designer — dimensions in, plans in 3 seconds
metadata.description: From Wooden Ren Academy. 25+ furniture templates. Enter dimensions, get three-view drawings, exploded 3D, joinery details, cut lists, build order, and printable PDFs. Built for DIYers, working woodworkers, designers, and students.
metadata.ogTitle: About · Wooden Ren Designer — dimensions in, plans in 3 seconds
metadata.ogDescription: From Wooden Ren Academy. 25+ templates, auto three-view, joinery, cut list, PDF plans.
metadata.twitterDescription: Dimensions in, plans / cut list / quote in 3 seconds. From Wooden Ren Academy.

hero.intro: Pick a piece, fill in length × width × height, and 3 seconds later you have a 3D view, three-view engineering drawings, joinery details, cut list, build order, and a printable PDF.
hero.triptychCaption: ↑ Same piece, three outputs generated in 3 seconds — 3D view, engineering drawings, cut list

triptych.panel1Alt: 3D view — walnut media console real output

mosaic.intro: Currently 25+ furniture templates — from pencil holders to wardrobes, all resize on the fly with sliders.
mosaic.outro: Just 12 of them shown — <link>see the full catalog</link>

pain.h2: The slow part of woodworking isn't building.
pain.intro: It's drawing the plan right, getting the stock math right, and sequencing the cuts. A stool plan typically eats 2–3 hours. A cabinet, half a day. Burned the afternoon before you even touch wood.

drawing.title: Drawing gets stuck
drawing.desc: Some dimension's always off — the three-view and the perspective just won't add up.

material.title: Stock math gets stuck
material.desc: Length × width × thickness, plus tenon allowance, plus waste, plus planing — half an hour goes counting on fingers.

process.title: Build order gets stuck
process.desc: What goes first, what gets glued, what gets clamped — get it wrong and you can't even take it apart to fix.

pain.conclusion: Designer compresses all three into 3 seconds — the two evenings you save are enough to pick up another commission.

steps.step1Desc: From 25+ furniture templates: stools, dining chairs, desks, wardrobes, wine racks, pencil holders — beginner through advanced.
steps.step2Desc: Length, width, height, stock thickness, joinery type — drag the sliders and watch the 3D update live.
steps.step3Title: Take the plans to the shop
steps.step3Desc: PDF engineering drawings, cut list, tool list, build order — print and start cutting.

outputs.h2: Every piece comes with all of this
outputs.intro: Not just a design — a complete build package, ready for the shop.

threeview.title: 📐 Engineering three-view
threeview.desc: Front, side, top — fully dimensioned, drawn in the conventions a working woodworker recognizes, print-and-build.

cutlist.title: 📋 Cut list
cutlist.desc: L × W × T, part number, 3D location for every piece — check them off as you cut, no mistakes.

cutplan.title: 🪵 Stock layout
cutplan.desc: Most efficient layout auto-computed with utilization percentage. Even tells you which side of the board to start the cut.

process.title: 📑 Build order
process.desc: Full 19-step sequence, time estimates, stock selection notes, moisture content, grain direction — more detailed than the textbook.

quote.title: 💰 Customer quote
quote.desc: Furniture price, tax, deposit / final split, installment terms, customer info table — print it and hand it to the customer.

perspective.title: 🪑 3D perspective preview
perspective.desc: Photoreal wood grain rendering, rotate to any angle — see the finished piece clearly before you cut.

joinery.title: Joinery details
joinery.desc: Through tenons, blind tenons, half-laps — positions auto-computed, with male/female alignment diagrams.

tools.title: Tool list
tools.desc: Which tools this piece needs — beginners don't have to guess what to buy.

stl.title: STL 3D export
stl.desc: Open in SketchUp or your 3D printer. Print a scale model to prototype before you commit.

decor.h2: Measure once, print the whole spec
decor.intro: Interior contractors burn most of their time not at the nail gun — but estimating on site with a pencil, drawing at home, then a second trip for parts. From ceiling frames to storage cabinets: enter the room dimensions, get the full spec.
decor.ceilingDesc: Measure the room and enter L×W×H — main joists, secondary joists, hangers auto-laid out. Length of every member, number of calcium silicate panels, number of nails, total stock and labor — print and bring to the job site.
decor.cabinetsTitle: Cabinet line — {count} common cabinet types, auto plans + cut list

persona.h2: Built for five kinds of people

diy.title: DIY woodworkers
diy.desc: Can't draft engineering drawings but want to build furniture for the home. Pick a template, adjust to your space, let the tool handle the rest.

pro.title: Pro woodworkers / commissions
pro.desc: Customer asks "how much for a cabinet like this?" — design + quote + build order in 5 minutes. Faster than the competition wins the job. Stock math precise so you don't over-order.

interior.title: Interior contractors / renovators
interior.desc: Measure the room and enter L×W×H — ceiling frame (main / secondary / supports) auto-computed, panels, nails, full spec to print and bring on site.

designer.title: Designers
designer.desc: Hand the engineering drawings and cut list straight to the woodworker for fabrication — minimum communication overhead.

student.title: Academy students / self-learners
student.desc: Change the dimensions or swap the joinery on a class project — see the 3D update instantly. Ten times faster than a textbook.

bio.p1: 200,000 people watch me work wood on YouTube. The Wooden Ren Academy, online and in person, has taught 600+ students.
bio.p2: The question I've gotten most often in ten years —
bio.quote: "Hey Ren, how do you figure out this dimension?"
bio.p3: Designer is the tool I built from those answers. Furniture sizing conventions, joinery rules, build-order logic — every single rule comes from work I've actually done and questions I've answered until they're worn smooth.

plans.h2: Try free, upgrade when you need to
plans.intro: Monthly from NT$390, yearly saves two months. One extra commission, or one stock-math mistake avoided — the subscription has already paid itself off.
plans.customTitle: 🪵 Not subscribing? Buy a single template
plans.customBody: Want to build just one chair? Buy that blueprint once and own it forever. No subscription required.
plans.customSeeAll: → See lifetime prices for all 26 templates
plans.perkNoCC: No credit card for free tier
plans.perkRetention: Cancel keeps access until period end
plans.bugReport: Hit a bug or stuck? Tap the Wooden Ren icon at the bottom right of any page (like the one on the left) to report it — it auto-attaches the current page and browser info, and I'll know how to fix it.

Enter dimensions, pick your wood, and get three-view drawings, exploded 3D, mortise & tenon details, cut lists, and printable PDFs. Free for the square stool and pencil holder; paid plans from $12.9/month.
metadata.ogTitle: Wooden Ren Designer — Plans in 3 seconds
metadata.ogDescription: Drawing, cut list, and quote compressed into 3 seconds. Free for the square stool and pencil holder. Built by the Wooden Ren woodworking academy.

hero.subtitleA: The slow part of woodworking isn't building — it's drawing the plan right, calculating the stock, and sequencing the cuts.
hero.subtitleB: This tool compresses all three into 3 seconds.
hero.note: No signup required to try · Square stool + pencil holder always free
hero.heroAlt: Wooden Ren Designer real output — walnut media console 3D perspective

pain.subtitle: Three frustrations we've heard from woodworkers for three years running.

drawing.title: I can picture it. I can't draw it.
drawing.body: You see it in your head — but how thick should the legs be? How deep the apron? Half an hour in SketchUp and you're still rotating the view.

material.title: Stock math is a nightmare
material.body: Board feet conversions, offcut waste estimation, a fair price that doesn't lose money — same math every single project.

quote.title: Quoting kills the deal
quote.body: A customer texts "how much for a dining table?" and you go home to do the math. Quote it in front of them — that's professional.

outputs.subtitle: Walk into the shop with the cut list and start sawing.

drawings.title: Three-view + joinery details
drawings.body: Front, side, top — auto-dimensioned. Through tenons / blind tenons / staggered half-laps positioned for you.

cutplan.title: Cut list + cutting diagram
cutplan.body: Auto-converted board feet, every piece sized and sequenced for minimum offcut waste.

quote.title: Quote + labor hours
quote.body: Stock × thickness × finishing × labor → total. Pro tier includes customer letterhead and margin.

templates.subtitle: Click any to see what it can do for you — design notes, real output, fit-for-purpose.

scenarios.subtitle: Six real scenarios — which one's most like you?

morning.body: On the couch with your phone, sketch a bedside table — frictionless, light, part of life.

workshop.body: Fresh PDF in hand against the stock — millimeter-precise, fewer rejects.

classroom.body: Every student opens Designer on their phone — abstract becomes concrete, joinery clicks instantly.

customer.body: Customer texts "can you build a dining table?" — pull up the design and iterate in the chat.

midnight.body: Inspiration doesn't wait. Half-asleep design now, refine tomorrow.

kid.body: 30 minutes with your kid designing a pencil holder — woodworking becomes a parent-child tool.

pricing.subtitle: Try free first. Upgrade when it clicks.

free.f4: Size cap: stool 35×35×45 cm, holder 20×20×25 cm

personal.f2: Ceiling frame + flooring + raised platform tools
personal.f4: For DIYers, home reno, weekend furniture builders

pricing.yearNote: Yearly billing saves over a month · Lifetime academy members DM for a code

custom.title: 🪵 Not subscribing? Buy a single template
custom.body: Just want to build one chair? Buy that blueprint once, own it forever. No subscription required.

faq.freeQ: What does the free tier actually include?
faq.freeA: The square stool and pencil holder — full 3D, joinery, three-view, cut list, PDF, exactly what paid users get. The difference is template count and size cap.
faq.cadQ: How does this compare to SketchUp / Fusion 360?
faq.cadA: Designer is for woodworkers who want to build, not learn CAD. Five-minute learning curve, automatic joinery / three-view / cut list / cutting plan. Not trying to replace professional CAD.
faq.tierQ: Personal vs Pro — what's the difference?
faq.tierA: Hobby use: Personal (full 26 templates + reno tools). Earning a living from woodworking: Pro (adds customer quote system, CNC export, unlimited size).
faq.cancelA: Yes. Monthly cancels anytime, keeps access through the current period. Yearly: 7-day no-questions full refund.
faq.cncQ: Can I take the output to CNC?
faq.cncA: Yes. Pro exports STL / OBJ for CNC and laser. Free and Personal three-view PDFs are sufficient for hand tools.

bottomCta.h2Highlight: Then decide if you want to pay.
bottomCta.subtitle: Fairest deal I know — use it first, pay only if it actually helps you.
